content_id:
[Kembali ke daftar isi] [60] Muslim dan Ibn Majah. Hadis ini mengandung petunjuk bahwa beliau tidak biasa memulainya dengan kata-kata sebagian orang: "Saya berniat untuk shalat..." yang sebenarnya disepakati sebagai bidah. Tetapi mereka berbeda tentang apakah itu bidah yang baik atau buruk, yang kita jawab: "Sesungguhnya semua bidah dalam ibadah adalah sesat, dari keumuman sabdanya alaihis shalatu was salam, dan semua bidah adalah menyesatkan, dan setiap yang menyesatkan ada di neraka." Tetapi ini bukan tempat untuk pembahasan rinci tentang hal ini. [61] Tabarani dengan isnad yang sahih. [62] Abu Dawud, Tirmizi, dan Hakim yang menyatakannya sahih dan Dhahabi menyetujui. Disebutkan dalam Irwa (no. 301). Secara harfiah, "takbir menjadikannya haram," yaitu tindakan-tindakan yang telah Allah haramkan selama shalat, "dan taslim menjadikannya halal," yaitu apa yang diizinkan di luar shalat. Sama seperti hadis membuktikan bahwa pintu shalat tertutup, tidak ada seorang pun yang dapat membukanya kecuali dengan bersuci, demikian pula membuktikan bahwa shalat tidak dapat dimasuki kecuali dengan takbir, dan tidak dapat diakhiri kecuali dengan taslim. Inilah pandangan mayoritas ulama. [63] Ahmad dan Hakim, yang menyatakannya sahih dan Dhahabi menyetujui. [64] Muslim dan Nasai. [65] Ahmad dan Baihaqi dengan isnad yang sahih. [66] Bukhari dan Nasai. [67] ibid. [68] Bukhari dan Abu Dawud. [69] Abu Dawud, Ibn Khuzaimah (1/62/2, 64/1), Tammam, dan Hakim yang menyatakannya sahih dan Dhahabi menyetujui. [70] Bukhari dan Nasai. [71] Bukhari dan Abu Dawud. [72] Muslim dan Abu Dawud. Juga disebutkan dalam Irwa (352). [73] Ibn Hibban dan Diya dengan isnad yang sahih. [74] Ahmad dan Abu Dawud dengan isnad yang sahih. [75] Abu Dawud, Nasai, dan Ibn Khuzaimah (1/54/2) dengan isnad yang sahih, dan Ibn Hibban menyatakannya sahih (485). [76] Malik, Bukhari, dan Abu Awanah. [77] Nasai dan Daraqutni dengan isnad yang sahih. Dalam hadis ini terdapat bukti bahwa menggenggam adalah sunnah, dan dalam hadis sebelumnya bahwa meletakkan juga sunnah, sehingga keduanya adalah sunnah. Adapun menggabungkan menggenggam dan meletakkan, yang dianggap baik oleh sebagian ulama Hanafi belakangan, maka itu adalah bidah; bentuknya sebagaimana mereka nyatakan adalah meletakkan tangan kanan di atas tangan kiri, menggenggam pergelangan tangan dengan kelingking dan ibu jari, dan meratakan tiga jari lainnya, sebagaimana dideskripsikan dalam Catatan Kaki Ibn Abidin pada Durr al-Mukhtar (1/454); maka janganlah bingung dengan apa yang mereka katakan.

[78] Abu Dawud, Ibn Khuzaimah dalam Sahih-nya (1/54/2), Ahmad, dan Abu Syaikh dalam Tarikh Isfahan (hal. 125); Tirmizi menyatakan salah satu isnadnya hasan, dan maknanya terdapat dalam al-Muwatta dan Sahih al-Bukhari jika diperhatikan dengan seksama. Saya telah mengutip lengkap isnad-isnad hadis ini dalam buku saya Ahkam al-Janaz (hal. 118). CATATAN: Meletakkan keduanya di atas dada adalah yang terbukti dalam Sunnah, dan semua yang bertentangan dengannya adalah daif atau sama sekali tidak berdasar. Bahkan, Imam Ishaq ibn Rahawayh mengamalkan sunnah ini, sebagaimana Marwazi berkata dalam Masail (hal. 222): "Ishaq biasa shalat witir bersama kami — ia mengangkat tangannya dalam qunut, dan melakukan qunut sebelum ruku, dan meletakkan tangannya di dadanya atau tepat di bawah dadanya." Serupa dengan ucapan Qadi Iyad al-Maliki dalam Mustahabbat as-Salah dalam bukunya al-Ilam (hal. 15, edisi ke-3, Rabat): "lengan kanan ditempatkan di punggung kiri, di bagian atas dada." Mendekati ini adalah apa yang Abdillah ibn Ahmad ibn Hanbal riwayatkan dalam Masailnya (hal. 62): "Saya melihat bahwa ketika shalat, ayah saya meletakkan tangannya, satu di atas yang lain, di atas pusar." Lihat Lampiran 4. [79] Bukhari dan Muslim. Disebutkan dalam Irwa (374) serta yang berikutnya. [80] Abu Dawud, Nasai, dan lainnya.
content_en:
[Back to contents] [60] Muslim & Ibn Maajah. The hadith contains an indication that he did not use to commence it with the words of some people: "I intend to pray _etc." which is in fact agreed to be an innovation. But they differ as to whether it is a good or bad innovation, to which we say: "Indeed all innovations in worship are misguided, from the generality of his statement (`alaihis salaatu was salaam), `_and all innovations are misleading, and every misleading thing is in the Fire'." But this is not the place for a detailed discussion of this. [61] Tabaraani with a sahih isnaad. [62] Abu Daawood, Tirmidhi and Haakim who declared it sahih and Dhahabi agreed. It is given in Irwaa' (no. 301). Literally, "the takbeer makes it haraam", i.e. the actions which Allaah has made haraam during it, "and the tasleem makes it halaal" i.e. what is allowed outside prayer. Just as the hadith proves that the door to prayer is shut, no worshipper being able to open it except with purification, it similarly proves that the prayer cannot be entered except with takbeer, and that it cannot be exited except with tasleem. This is the view of the majority of scholars. [63] Ahmad & Haakim, who declared it sahih and Dhahabi agreed. [64] Muslim & Nasaa'i. [65] Ahmad & Baihaqi with a sahih isnaad. [66] Bukhaari & Nasaa'i. [67] ibid. [68] Bukhaari & Abu Daawood. [69] Abu Daawood, Ibn Khuzaimah (1/62/2, 64/1), Tammaam & Haakim who declared it sahih and Dhahabi agreed. [70] Bukhaari & Nasaa'i. [71] Bukhaari & Abu Daawood. [72] Muslim & Abu Daawood. It is also given in Irwaa' (352). [73] Ibn Hibbaan & Diyaa', with a sahih isnaad. [74] Ahmad & Abu Daawood, with a sahih isnaad. [75] Abu Daawood, Nasaa'i & Ibn Khuzaimah (1/54/2) with a sahih isnaad, and Ibn Hibbaan declared it sahih (485). [76] Maalik, Bukhaari & Abu `Awaanah. [77] Nasaa'i and Daaraqutni with a sahih isnaad. In this hadith there is evidence that grasping is from the sunnah, and in the previous hadith that so is placing, so both are sunnah. As for the combination of holding and placing, which some of the later Hanafis hold to be good, then that is an innovation; its form as they state is to place the right hand on the left, holding the wrist with the little

finger and the thumb, and laying flat the remaining three fingers, as described in Ibn `Aabideen's Footnotes on Durr al-Mukhtaar (1/454); so do not be confused by what they say. [78] Abu Daawood, Ibn Khuzaimah in his sahih (1/54/2), Ahmad & Abu Shaikh in Taareekh Isbahaan (p. 125); Tirmidhi declared one of its isnaads hasan, and its meaning is found in Al-Muwatta' and sahih Al-Bukhaari if considered carefully. I have fully quoted the isnaads of this hadith in my book Ahkaam al-Janaa'iz (p. 118) NOTE: To place them on the chest is what is proved in the Sunnah, and all that is contrary to it is either da`eef or totally baseless. In fact, Imaam Ishaaq ibn Raahawaih acted on this sunnah, as Marwazi said in Masaa'il (p. 222): "Ishaaq used to pray witr with us _he would raise his hands in qunoot, and make the qunoot before bowing, and place his hands on his breast or just under his breast." Similar is the saying of Qaadi `Iyaad al-Maaliki in Mustahabbaat as-Salaah in his book al-I`laam (p.15, 3rd edition, Rabat): "the right arm is to be placed on the back of the left, on the upper part of the chest." Close to this is what `Abdullaah ibn Ahmad ibn Hanbal related in his Masaa'il (p. 62): "I saw that when praying, my father placed his hands, one on the other, above the navel." See Appendix 4. [79] Bukhaari & Muslim. It is given in Irwaa' (374) as well as the following one. [80] Abu Daawood, Nasaa'i and others.
content_ar: [Back to contents] [60] مسلم & Ibn Maajah. The الحديث contains an indication that he did not use to commence it with the words of some people: "I intend to pray _etc." which is in fact agreed to be an البدعة. But they differ as to whether it is a الخير or bad البدعة, to which we say: "Indeed all innovations in worship are misguided, from the generality of his statement (`alaihis salaatu was salaam), `_and all innovations are misleading, and every misleading thing is in the Fire'." But this is not the place for a detailed discussion of this. [61] Tabaraani with a صحيح isnaad. [62] Abu Daawood, الترمذي and Haakim who declared it صحيح and Dhahabi agreed. It is given in Irwaa' (no. 301). Literally, "the takbeer makes it haraam", i.e. the actions which Allaah has made haraam خلال it, "and the tasleem makes it halaal" i.e. what is allowed outside الصلاة. Just as the الحديث proves that the door to الصلاة is shut, no worshipper being able to open it except with purification, it similarly proves that the الصلاة cannot be entered except with takbeer, and that it cannot be exited except with tasleem. This is the view of the majority of العلماء. [63] أحمد & Haakim, who declared it صحيح and Dhahabi agreed. [64] مسلم & Nasaa'i. [65] أحمد & Baihaqi with a صحيح isnaad. [66] Bukhaari & Nasaa'i. [67] ibid. [68] Bukhaari & Abu Daawood. [69] Abu Daawood, Ibn Khuzaimah (1/62/2, 64/1), Tammaam & Haakim who declared it صحيح and Dhahabi agreed. [70] Bukhaari & Nasaa'i. [71] Bukhaari & Abu Daawood. [72] مسلم & Abu Daawood. It is أيضاً given in Irwaa' (352). [73] Ibn Hibbaan & Diyaa', with a صحيح isnaad. [74] أحمد & Abu Daawood, with a صحيح isnaad. [75] Abu Daawood, Nasaa'i & Ibn Khuzaimah (1/54/2) with a صحيح isnaad, and Ibn Hibbaan declared it صحيح (485). [76] Maalik, Bukhaari & Abu `Awaanah. [77] Nasaa'i and Daaraqutni with a صحيح isnaad. In this الحديث there is evidence that grasping is from the السنة, and in the previous الحديث that لذا is placing, لذا both are السنة. أما the combination of holding and placing, which some of the later Hanafis hold to be الخير, then that is an البدعة; its form as they state is to place the الحق hand on the left, holding the wrist with the little finger and the thumb, and laying flat the remaining three fingers, as described in Ibn `Aabideen's Footnotes on Durr al-Mukhtaar (1/454); لذا do not be confused by what they say. [78] Abu Daawood, Ibn Khuzaimah in his صحيح (1/54/2), أحمد & Abu Shaikh in Taareekh Isbahaan (p. 125); الترمذي declared one of its isnaads حسن, and its meaning is found in Al-الموطأ' and صحيح Al-Bukhaari إذا considered carefully. I have fully quoted the isnaads of this الحديث in my الكتاب Ahkaam al-Janaa'iz (p. 118) NOTE: To place them on the chest is what is proved in the السنة, and all that is contrary to it is either da`eef or totally baseless. In fact, Imaam Ishaaq ibn Raahawaih acted on this السنة, as Marwazi said in Masaa'il (p. 222): "Ishaaq used to pray witr w
